filter_pushdown.note: l_shipdate is sorted ascending — binary search on zone map to find first qualifying block (max_val >= 8766) and last qualifying block (min_val < 9131)
l_discount zone map provides no skipping — all blocks span [0.00, 0.10]. Apply as row-level filter only.
l_quantity zone map provides no skipping — all blocks span [1, 50]. Apply as row-level filter only.

filter_application_order: l_shipdate (block-level zone map skip first, then row-level; cheapest/most selective)
l_discount (row-level, 27% pass rate — applied second to candidate rows)
l_quantity (row-level, 48% pass rate — applied third to further reduce survivors)
l_extendedprice (loaded last via late materialization; only for ~1.8% of rows that pass all filters)

late_materialization.reason: Only ~1.1M of 9M scanned rows pass all three filters. Load l_extendedprice only for surviving rows to minimize memory bandwidth.

data_structures.note: No hash tables needed. Each thread accumulates a local double `partial_sum`. Final result is a single reduction across threads.

parallelism.blocks_per_thread: ~1-2 (use dynamic scheduling: each thread grabs the next unprocessed block)
parallelism.merge_strategy: sum all thread-local partial_sum values after parallel phase — O(nthreads) sequential step
parallelism.note: With only ~90 qualifying blocks and 64 threads, use dynamic block assignment (std::atomic<int> next_block) to avoid load imbalance from variable block fill rates near zone map boundaries.

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then [double min_val, double max_val, uint32_t row_count, uint32_t _pad] per block (24 bytes/entry)
index_usage.skip_condition: skip block i if entry[i].max_val < 8766.0 OR entry[i].min_val >= 9131.0
index_usage.optimization: binary search for first block with max_val >= 8766 and last block with min_val < 9131; scan only that contiguous range of ~90 blocks
index_usage.block_row_offset: block i starts at row i * 100000 (direct array index into col_ptr)

scan_detail.pass_1: Load l_shipdate_zonemap (14KB) once. Identify qualifying block range via binary search. Build list of ~90 block IDs.
pass_2_per_block: Load l_shipdate chunk (100K × 4 bytes = 400KB). Apply row-level filter: 8766 <= val < 9131. Build row bitmask or compact index.
Load l_discount chunk. For candidate rows only: apply 0.05-1e-9 <= val <= 0.07+1e-9. Narrow candidate set.
Load l_quantity chunk. For remaining candidates: apply val < 24.0. Narrow further.
Load l_extendedprice chunk. For final survivors (~1.8%): accumulate partial_sum += extprice[i] * discount[i].

physical_plan.memory_detail: Each column is read sequentially within each qualifying block. All four column chunks for a block fit in L3 cache (4 × 100K × 8B = 3.2MB per block vs 44MB L3). Prefetch next block's l_shipdate while processing current block's later columns.

estimated_cost.target_comparison: Umbra=14ms — plan is designed to match via zone-map block skip + late materialization + 64-thread block parallelism
